ingredients.item:
Lotus stems, or rootlets, come from lotus flowers. Their crunchy, yet tender, texture is perfect for salads and stir-fried dishes. You can find them pickled in brine in glass jars in the canned food section of most Asian grocery stores. I also use fried lotus roots (the chubby, underground stems, or rhizomes) in this salad to diversify the texture and add some interesting visuals to the presentation.
SKILL LEVELBasic|YIELD3 or 4 servings
10½ ounces (300 g) pickled lotus stems
1 small carrot, peeled and shredded
2 tablespoons sugar
Pinch of salt
2 teaspoons vegetable or mushroom stock powder, plus more to taste
3½ ounces (100 g) lotus root, peeled
3 tablespoons all-purpose flour
½ cup (120 ml) vegetable oil
ingredients.original:
Lotus stems, or rootlets, come from lotus flowers. Their crunchy, yet tender, texture is perfect for salads and stir-fried dishes. You can find them pickled in brine in glass jars in the canned food section of most Asian grocery stores. I also use fried lotus roots (the chubby, underground stems, or rhizomes) in this salad to diversify the texture and add some interesting visuals to the presentation.
SKILL LEVELBasic|YIELD3 or 4 servings
10½ ounces (300 g) pickled lotus stems
1 small carrot, peeled and shredded
2 tablespoons sugar
Pinch of salt
2 teaspoons vegetable or mushroom stock powder, plus more to taste
3½ ounces (100 g) lotus root, peeled
3 tablespoons all-purpose flour
½ cup (120 ml) vegetable oil

ingredients.item: ¼ ounce (7 g) dried, seasoned laver seaweed (see Note)
ingredients.original: ¼ ounce (7 g) dried, seasoned laver seaweed (see Note)

ingredients.item: 1 ounce (28 g) mint leaves, roughly chopped
ingredients.original: 1 ounce (28 g) mint leaves, roughly chopped

ingredients.item: 1 ounce (28 g) Vietnamese mint, roughly chopped
ingredients.original: 1 ounce (28 g) Vietnamese mint, roughly chopped

ingredients.item: 1. Rinse lotus stems well, drain, and place in a large bowl. Add the carrot, sugar, salt, and stock powder, and then toss well. Set aside for 10 minutes to allow the lotus stems to absorb all the seasonings.
ingredients.original: 1. Rinse lotus stems well, drain, and place in a large bowl. Add the carrot, sugar, salt, and stock powder, and then toss well. Set aside for 10 minutes to allow the lotus stems to absorb all the seasonings.

ingredients.item: 2. With a sharp knife or mandoline, cut the lotus roots crosswise into ⅛-inch (3 mm) thick slices.
ingredients.original: 2. With a sharp knife or mandoline, cut the lotus roots crosswise into ⅛-inch (3 mm) thick slices.

ingredients.item: 3. Put flour on a plate, coat lotus root slices in it, and shake off excess.
ingredients.original: 3. Put flour on a plate, coat lotus root slices in it, and shake off excess.

ingredients.item: 4. In a medium skillet, heat oil over medium heat. When hot, carefully drop in lotus root slices and fry until golden brown on both sides (about 2 minutes per side).
ingredients.original: 4. In a medium skillet, heat oil over medium heat. When hot, carefully drop in lotus root slices and fry until golden brown on both sides (about 2 minutes per side).

ingredients.item: 5. Using a slotted spoon, transfer lotus root slices to a paper towel–lined plate to drain.
ingredients.original: 5. Using a slotted spoon, transfer lotus root slices to a paper towel–lined plate to drain.

ingredients.item: 6. In a large bowl, combine lotus stems, fried lotus root slices, seaweed, mint, and Vietnamese mint. Toss well, taste, and add more stock powder as needed.
ingredients.original: 6. In a large bowl, combine lotus stems, fried lotus root slices, seaweed, mint, and Vietnamese mint. Toss well, taste, and add more stock powder as needed.

ingredients.item: 7. Transfer salad to bowls and serve.
ingredients.original: 7. Transfer salad to bowls and serve.
